contract_conformance.notes: Backend implementation conforms to contracts/event-log.md (v1.0.0), db-schema.sql (v1.1.0), and action-protocol.schema.json (v1.1.0). Verified by behavior on MockProvider: (1) events table is append-only -- seq is AUTOINCREMENT, strictly increasing, dense per run; the only INSERT INTO events is save_event's append, and there is NO 'UPDATE events' / 'DELETE FROM events' anywhere in repository.py (asserted statically with a passing negative control). (2) The section 3 decision-trace chain is emitted in exact order turn_start->perceived->memory_retrieved->llm_call->reasoning->action_chosen->(domain event[s])->action_resolved, all under one turn_id, ordered by seq via get_turn_trace; multi-event actions (vote->rule_passed) and domain events inherit the turn_id. (3) llm_call payload carries the exact OTel GenAI key set (gen_ai.request.model, gen_ai.response.model, gen_ai.usage.input_tokens, gen_ai.usage.output_tokens, latency_ms, gen_ai.response.finish_reasons, cached, attempt). (4) EM-066 fields (perceived_summary/memories_used/reasoning) surface in perceived/memory_retrieved/reasoning rows, and are optional-safe (absent => null, valid chain). (5) Read API get_events filters (kinds, actor_id, turn_id, from/to_tick, after_seq, limit, order asc|desc, default asc), get_rule_history (proposed->passed with votes + all documented keys), and get_analytics (all 11 documented top-level keys incl. by_model + usage.by_profile) behave per section 7.

invariants.notes: All W5 gate invariants hold under deterministic MockProvider runs. APPEND-ONLY: seq strictly increasing with no gaps reused; re-reading an earlier seq range after more turns returns byte-identical rows (no mutation path). STAMPING: every row has sim_time == round(tick*tick_interval_seconds,3) and a non-null actor_type, correctly human_agent for agent turns and system for actor-less engine/random injections. SNAPSHOTS+REPLAY (headline): snapshots written at tick 0 and exactly at every snapshot_interval_ticks (no off-cadence rows); with interval=1 the independently-written nearest_snapshot(T).state reproduces the live world.to_snapshot() positions/energy/credits at T across 15+ ticks (real equality on divergent state -- distinct locations + energy across agents); fold-forward variant seeds from a coarser snapshot@10 and folds economy{credits_delta} rows over get_events forward to T=13 to match live per-agent credits exactly (boundary verified: snapshot at tick K precedes events stamped tick K, so fold window is [base_tick, T-1]).

test_coverage.notes: Added backend/tests/test_event_log.py: 16 new pytest cases, deterministic and offline (MockProvider + TickLoop._execute_turn), one per W5 gate invariant family -- append-only (3), turn chain (2), stamping (1), OTel llm_call keys (1), decision trace incl. optional-safe (2), snapshots+replay determinism incl. fold-forward (3), read API get_events/get_rule_history/get_analytics (4). Mirrors existing tests/*.py harness patterns (World/Router/Runtime/loop wiring). Full suite: 79 passed (63 prior + 16 new), 0 failed. Frontend event-log consumers are W6 and out of scope for this gate.

security.notes: Low surface, local-first, no auth/PII. Strong points relevant to this contract: all event reads are parameterized SQL (kinds IN-clause and every filter use bound '?' params -- no string-built SQL exposed to model/user output); model output remains JSON-schema + world-rule validated before any event is written; the append-only design means a compromised consumer cannot tamper with the source-of-truth log (no UPDATE/DELETE path exists). No new attack surface introduced by the test additions. Not hardened for public-internet exposure (documented as local-first).

issues: llm_call usage tokens (gen_ai.usage.input_tokens / output_tokens) are null under W5 -- providers do not capture token counts until W6 (EM-067). The KEYS are present and asserted; only the values are null. This matches the contract (usage int|null) and is not a defect.

The fold-forward replay assertion required pinning the snapshot/tick boundary: a snapshot written at tick K reflects state BEFORE events stamped tick==K (the snapshot is taken by the turn that incremented world.tick to K). Tests encode this boundary explicitly so future cadence changes stay correct.

Engine-side replay is verified via the snapshot+fold equivalence the contract guarantees (nearest_snapshot+get_events == live projection); there is no public repo.replay(T) convenience method, so the fold is performed in-test. Adding repo.replay(T) would be a nice-to-have but is not required by event-log.md section 7.

gate_decision.reason: All 79 tests pass (16 new W5 gate tests + 63 existing regressions), zero failures, zero blockers. No CRITICAL blocker. contract_conformance=5 and security=4 both clear the >=3 thresholds; no new or existing test fails. The headline replay-determinism guarantee (snapshot + fold-forward == live world projection at T) is asserted as a real equality on divergent agent state, append-only is enforced both behaviorally and statically (no UPDATE/DELETE path on events), the OTel llm_call key set is exact, and the read API (get_events filters, get_rule_history, get_analytics) conforms to event-log.md section 7. W5 gate is GREEN.
